doc: Equal-power crossfade between two self-playing tones, built entirely from math operators (no crossfade Operator). Source A is a 220 Hz sine, source B a 330 Hz saw (a fifth up, different timbre) so the blend is obvious by ear with no OSC sender. The fade follows out = A*(1-t)^k + B*t^k. Two knobs: /xfade/in [0..1] sweeps the position (rests at 0 = full A), smoothed by an m2s so it is zipper-free; /fade_law/in sets the fade exponent k and is the A/B switch — 0.5 = equal-power (sqrt law, constant power, sqrt(1-t)^2 + sqrt(t)^2 = 1), 1.0 = linear. Default is 0.5 (equal power). The summed mix is attenuated 0.5 to leave headroom. This is the inline pattern meant to lift straight into a `crossfader` sub-patch once instrument nesting (ADR-0034) lands.

nodes.doc: Source B: a 330 Hz saw (a fifth above A, brighter timbre).
Crossfade position knob t in [0..1]; rests at 0 (full A). m2s smooths it so sweeping is zipper-free. Send /xfade/in [0..1].
The fade exponent k, fanned to both gain curves so one message changes both. 0.5 = equal-power (default), 1.0 = linear. Send /fade_law/in 1.0 or /fade_law/in 0.5.
1 - t, the complementary fade for source A.
Gain for A = (1 - t)^k. k=0.5 gives sqrt(1-t) (equal power), k=1 gives 1-t (linear).
Source A scaled by its fade gain.
Source B scaled by its fade gain.
Sum of the two faded sources: A*(1-t)^k + B*t^k.
Halve the mix for headroom (two sources near unity at the equal-power center).
